title: Local Gift Cards
description: What are multi-store gift cards that support local businesses and can be used across various merchants in a specific town or region?
narrative: The user seeks information about physical or digital gift cards that are redeemable at multiple independent businesses within a defined local area, such as a town or city center. Relevance is determined by whether the gift card is issued by a municipal or merchant consortium (e.g., 'Coeur de Ville'), supports flexible spending across diverse retailers (restaurants, boutiques, services), enables incremental usage (e.g., in €10 increments), and promotes local economic activity. Documents describing single-merchant gift cards (e.g., restaurant-only or Viking jewelry store cards), digital-only cards without local merchant networks, or unrelated products like Magic: The Gathering cards are not relevant. The ideal solution combines community-based distribution, URSSAF-compliant business incentives, and multi-vendor usability.

title: Potato Cultivation
description: What are the most effective, low-effort methods for growing and storing potatoes that maximize yield with minimal labor?
narrative: The user seeks practical, time-efficient potato-growing techniques that prioritize ease of execution and high return on effort, particularly for home gardeners with limited time or physical capacity. Relevant documents describe methods such as leaving tubers in place over winter, using compost lasagna beds, mulching with straw or grass clippings, planting in pallets or raised beds, and growing from peelings or sprouted store-bought potatoes—techniques that reduce tilling, weeding, and harvesting labor. The user is not interested in commercial farming equipment, sweet potato recipes, or potato storage in industrial settings, but rather in home-scale, low-input, sustainable practices that mimic ‘lazy gardening’ while still producing reliable harvests. Relevance is determined by the presence of hands-on, experiential advice from gardeners who have successfully minimized effort without sacrificing yield.

title: Senior Return-to-Work Incentives
description: What financial support and incentives are available for unemployed individuals aged 50 and over who want to return to work in Belgium or France?
narrative: The user is seeking detailed information on government-sponsored programs that encourage re-employment for older workers, particularly those aged 50+. Relevant documents must describe specific monetary supplements (e.g., return-to-work supplement, income guarantee allowance, senior holidays allowance), eligibility criteria (e.g., age, years of prior service, part-time vs. full-time work), and how these benefits interact with existing unemployment payments or pensions. Documents discussing bonuses for employers hiring seniors (e.g., €2,000 incentive in France) are also relevant. Irrelevant documents include those about energy renovation grants, tax reforms, slot machines, or teacher mobility schemes, as they do not address employment incentives for seniors returning to the workforce.
